title: Playbook - Microsoft Graph SharePoint Site Discovery And Resolution
applies_to: Finding and resolving SharePoint sites before list or file operations
prerequisites: Working Graph token with site-read capability
One of: known hostname and path, known group ID, known site IDs, or free-text search keywords
required_permissions: Site discovery APIs generally require Sites.Read.All or stronger permissions for app-only access
Use higher scopes only when later CRUD operations require them
request_sequence: If the exact site path is known, resolve it directly: GET /sites/{hostname}:/{relative-path}.
If only a group or Team is known, resolve the group site: GET /groups/{group-id}/sites/root.
If a site ID is already known, get it directly: GET /sites/{site-id}.
If only keywords are known, search sites across the tenant: GET /sites?search={query}.
If broad inventory is required, enumerate sites: GET /sites/getAllSites.
If the target may be a subsite, enumerate children: GET /sites/{site-id}/sites.
After discovery, cache the canonical site ID and use it for downstream drive and list operations.

confirmed_facts.fact: The SharePoint API root resources include /sites/root, /sites/{site-id}, /sites/{site-id}/drive, /sites/{site-id}/drives, /sites/{site-id}/sites, /sites/{site-id}/lists, and /groups/{group-id}/sites/root.
Sites can be resolved by path using /sites/{hostname}:/{relative-path}.
Site search supports free-text search across a SharePoint tenant with GET /sites?search={query}, and the only sortable property is createdDateTime.
Tenant-wide site enumeration is available through getAllSites.
Subsites can be enumerated with GET /sites/{site-id}/sites.

implementation_notes: Prefer path-based resolution when the exact URL is known because it avoids ambiguous search results.
Use search for interactive discovery by name or keyword, and getAllSites only for inventory-style synchronization or full-tenant exploration.
Resolve once, then cache the site ID and related drive or list IDs for later CRUD operations.
When mapping old SharePoint REST or CSOM patterns, remember that Graph site IDs encode both site collection and web identifiers.
common_failures: Search returning multiple similarly named sites and the caller choosing the wrong one.
404 because the relative path omits a subsite segment.
Overusing getAllSites for interactive lookups when path resolution or search is cheaper and more precise.
